course_title: Anthropology: Special Topics: The Art of Early China
subject: ANT
course_description: Seminar or lecture series on topics of anthropological interest at an intermediate level. Maybe repeated for credit when topic varies.
course_notes: (Same as ARTHIST 289W 1, CHN 285W 1) "The Art of Early China"What can ceramic, jade, bone, bronze, stone, or silk tell us about life and history in early China? How might modern archaeology complicate the traditional descriptions of Chinese culture? In what ways do contemporary biases inform our interpretations of the ancient material remains? This course explores significant works of art from the Neolithic period, the Bronze Age, and the early imperial periods (ca. 200 CE). We will closely analyze objects unearthed through archaeological excavations and compare them with related historical texts. Readings drawn from both academic scholarship and popular literature will complement our weekly lectures and discussions. Art History major / minor: Div. I or IV
